author: codex-agent

patch.value: Validate UUID fields at telemetry ingestion boundary

Remote telemetry worker logs repeatedly show worker_insert_failed invalid input syntax for type uuid when events contain non-UUID installation_id/session_id/event_id values. Invalid payloads should be rejected (or sanitized/quarantined) before queue insert to prevent retry churn and queue pollution.
Ingestion path validates UUID-typed identifiers before queueing; invalid payloads are dropped/quarantined with explicit counters and do not repeatedly fail worker inserts.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Duplicate-check evidence: pm context --limit 10; pm search 'telemetry worker invalid uuid' --limit 10; pm list-open --limit 20; pm list-in-progress --limit 20. Net-new because current telemetry open items cover docs/health visibility but not ingestion boundary UUID validation and worker retry churn.

message: Track telemetry invalid UUID ingestion gap

value.note: Remote telemetry stack runbook used during verification
Telemetry runtime and search caches excluded from git
Client-side telemetry event shaping and redaction

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Remote verification evidence 2026-04-26: docker compose services healthy on steve@192.168.1.183 (telemetry-api/worker/rabbitmq/postgres/prometheus/loki/tempo/alloy/grafana); /healthz returned ok queue pm.telemetry.events; synthetic POST /v1/events accepted=1; Postgres telemetry_events count increased (9516->9519) and live command ingest confirmed (stats delta +1); DSAR export/delete scripts executed successfully for probe installation UUID. Outstanding failure signal: telemetry-worker logs repeated worker_insert_failed invalid UUID errors for non-UUID identifiers, indicating ingestion boundary lacks strict UUID validation before worker insert.
